The years of the 8th lustrum owned by Visve-devas are
Sobhakrt or Sokahrt, Subhakrt, Krodhin, Visvavasu and Parnbhava.
The first two years will be highly beneficial to the people, while
the third very troublesome. The last two will be moderate,
but the last one, Parabhava, will be afflicted with fire, weapons,
diseases, sorrow, and danger to Brahmins and cattle.
[For there is a variant reading viz. According
to tradition the year Subhakrt precedes Sobhakrt. The metre is
upto the 45th verse.]

In the 9th lustrum belonging to the Moon the five years are
in order Plavanga, Kjlaka, Saumya, Sadharana and Rodhakjt. The two
years, Ktlaka and Saumya, are auspicious and beneficial, while
Plavahga is troublesome to the people in many ways. In the year
